# Search Trees

## BST (Binary Search Tree)
### Definition
1. Order property: all keys in left subtree < root < all keys in right subtree
2. A **key** is the value stored in each node that is used for ordering the tree and making search decisions.
3. Each node has a key *k*
### Key operations
- Searching: search by key
- Inserting into
- Deleting items

## Priority queues and heaps
Priority Queue; [Heap](heap.md)

## Balanced Searching
> a bushy binary tree has a height of log(N) but its not guaranteed and can end up spindly which is O(N)
- [B-Trees](b-trees.md); [Red_Black Trees](red-black-trees.md)
- Tries
